title: Deguelia subavenis
intro: Deguelia subavenis, also known as "Tahuari," is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Leguminosae (Fabaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Deguelia subavenis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as deguelin and 4-phenylcoumarin, which exhibit potential antioxidant and anticancer activities. In traditional medicine, Deguelia subavenis is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Deguelia subavenis is classified as a species of plant in the Fabaceae family. This family of plants is commonly known as legumes. Deguelia subavenis is further classified as a member of the genus Deguelia. The genus Deguelia is part of the subfamily Faboideae. Further research is needed to confirm the specific classification of Deguelia subavenis.
